name: Yoga

Experience a fun and light-hearted, mid-intensity flow that will move you seamlessly from Sun Salutation to a final peak pose. name: NYRR Open Run: Canarsie Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Canarsie Park serves the neighborhoods of Canarsie and nearby Flatlands, Mill Basin, Marine Park, and Bergen Beach. Home to baseball fields and cricket pitches, it’s bordered by the Belt Parkway and Paerdegat Basin.

name: NYRR Open Run: St. Marys Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.St. Mary's Park offers a wonderful respite from the hustle and bustle of urban life. Located in the Mott Haven neighborhood in the South Bronx, the park features rolling hills, expanses of green grass, and grand old trees. The site was once part of the estate of Jonas Bronck (1600-43), for whom the Bronx is named. It is one of the largest parks in the area with a pool, playground, basketball courts, an indoor recreation center, picnic seating areas, and more. In the winter, the hills are perfect for sledding. St. Mary’s Park received $30 million from NYC’s Anchor Parks initiative, which will renovate the park’s ballfields, pathways, and amphitheater.

name: NYRR Open Run: Inwood Hill Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Evidence of Inwood Hill Park’s formation exists as caves, valleys, and ridges left as the result of shifting glaciers. Evidence of its uninhabited state afterward remains as its forest and salt marsh (the last natural one in Manhattan), and evidence of its use by Native Americans continues to be discovered. Most of the park was largely untouched by wars and development. The park features a hiking trail and the Hudson River Bike Trail, and is one of the best places in NYC to spot a bald eagle. It also includes athletic fields, playgrounds, dog runs, and a barbecue area.The course is three loops on hilly trails and walkways through woods and along a salt marsh.
location_name: Seaman Avenue and Isham Street Entrance (in Inwood Hill Park)

name: NYRR Open Run: Baisley Pond Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Baisley Pond Park is located near JFK Airport in Springfield Gardens, Queens on the border of NYC and Nassau County. The park features tennis, handball, and basketball courts; peaceful, shady alcoves for picnicking; and plenty of plant and animal wildlife.
location_name: Baisley Pond Park House (in Baisley Pond Park)

name: NYRR Open Run: Crocheron Park

The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Widely known for its history with the Crocheron Family and summer house, Crocheron Park is home to running paths, baseball fields, and a 4-court tennis center.
location_name: Entrance to John Golden Park (in John Golden Park)

name: NYRR Open Run: Highland Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Located in the Cypress Hills section of Brooklyn, Highland Park is close to East New York and Bushwick in Brooklyn and the Woodhaven, Ridgewood, and Glendale neighborhoods in Queens. It’s home to over 150 species of birds and to the Ridgewood Reservoir.
location_name: Elton Street and Jamaica Avenue (in Highland Park)

name: NYRR Open Run: Shore Road Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Shore Road Park, located in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn, is one of our longer Open Run courses. The park serves residents of Bay Ridge, Sunset Park, Dyker Heights, Fort Hamilton, and other surrounding neighborhoods.The course is three loops alongside Shore Road with stunning views of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge.
location_name: Lawn Near 79th Street Entrance (in Shore Park and Parkway)

name: NYRR Open Run: Soundview Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Called the “Gateway to the Bronx River,” 205-acre Soundview Park is located where the Bronx River opens into the East River. This urban green space is filled with grassy baseball and soccer fields, a cricket pitch, basketball courts, a running track, walking/biking paths, picnic fields, and more! With its extensive views of the water, Soundview Park celebrates the beauty of both the Bronx and East Rivers. The back part of our Open Run course has beautiful views of the Bronx River waterfront for participants to enjoy!
location_name: Entrance - Lafayette Avenue and Morrison Avenue (in Soundview Park)

name: NYRR Open Run: Astoria Park Dog Days of Summer
description: Bring your 4-legged friends to a special Open Run! NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels.Astoria Park is home to the oldest and largest pool in New York City. The park features views of Manhattan and a newly renovated track funded by the Anchor Parks initiative.

name: No Excuses Circuit Training
description: A high-energy beginner-friendly workout that combines strength and endurance exercises performed in timed stations with minimal rest. Participants rotate through movements targeting different muscle groups to improve fitness. Our goals are to increase stamina, build strength, and enhance overall body conditioning in an efficient, full-body training session while connecting with your community! Participants must sign in at the front desk and bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.

name: It's My Park at Westerleigh Park
description: This It's My Park season, volunteer with Westerleigh Parks Coalition to rake leaves and clean debris around Westerleigh Park. We look forward to cleaning up the park with you!

name: It's My Park at Wolfe's Pond Park
description: This It's My Park season, volunteer with The New York City Mountain Bike Association to rake, cut back thorn bushes and other overgrowth, clear natural debris, and pick up litter at Wolfe's Pond Park! Volunteers help conduct our monthly trail inspection, perform routine maintenance tasks, such as litter pickup raking, erosion control(digging) and signage maintenance. Volunteer hours start at 9:00am rain or shine. Optional group off-road mountain bike ride at 12:30pm on the trails (The Route: mostly beginner friendly; Distance: ~7 miles). Both groups meet at the Maintenance Shop in the Main Parking lot at Wolfe's Pond Park 475 Cornelia Ave Staten Island, NY 10312.

name: CANCELED: Soca Dance Fitness

name: Dance Fitness- Upbeat Pop!

Whether you want to learn some new choreography or need a space to step-touch and sing, this class is for you!

name: Yoga in the Park
description: Stretch, breathe, and find your inner peace at Randall’s Island Park’s free outdoor yoga series. Guided by a beloved, local yoga instructor, participants will flow through a calming and grounding sequence against the backdrop of the uptown skyline. All levels and abilities are welcome. Bring a friend, family member or two, or your yoga-loving pet! No registration required—just show up ready to move! We’ll have water and a limited number of mats available to borrow, so please bring your own if possible or show up early to claim one. Our free yoga classes run weekly on Tuesdays from 6:30 – 7:30 pm from May 5 – September 1, and Saturdays from 10:00 – 11:00 am, July 11 – August 29. On Saturdays, stay after and join our dance class! Please check Instagram for the most updated schedule. Location: East River Picnic Area For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation, or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.
location_name: East River Picnic Area (in Randall's Island Park)

name: Cross Country Course Volunteer Days
description: Be a part of the community effort to maintain the Cross Country Trail in Van Cortlandt Park. Whether you’re an experienced trailblazer or new to trail maintenance, your help will make a significant impact!Event Dates and Times:First Saturdays of the Month from May to September from 10am to 1pm.* *Please note that for groups of 10 or more only. Please email garcialeslie@vancortlandt.org to schedule a group.*Please note that due to the July 4th Holiday, the July event has been moved to the second Saturday. Meeting Details:Location: Van Cortlandt Park, Garden and Education Compost Site, across from Riverdale Stables.Time: 10 am sharp; work begins at 10:15 amUpcoming Activities Include:Clearing debris and overgrowthRepairing trail structuresEnsuring trail safety and accessibility What to Bring:Comfortable, weather-appropriate clothingClosed-toed shoesWater and a snackWhy Join Us?Contribute to the vitality and safety of the trails you love! Engage with fellow community members, learn about trail maintenance, and enjoy the satisfaction of hands-on work in the great outdoors.Necessary tools and gloves will be provided!Want to bring a group? This event is limited to small groups of 9 or less. If you would like to bring more 10 or more people please reach out to garcialeslie@vancortlandt.org to schedule a separate event.
location_name: VCPA Garden & Compost Site (in Van Cortlandt Park)

name: August Project WASTE
description: Join us for our upcoming Project WASTE event! This event is part of our hands-on outreach strategy which includes trash collection and assessment.

name: Volunteer Day: Bay 84th Street Community Garden
description: GreenThumb community gardens are developed, led, and cared for by dedicated volunteer gardeners who critically upkeep them as public green spaces.Bay 84th Street Community Garden, is searching for its community! We invite you to come visit the garden and help reimagine what this vital green space can be. We will be hosting an open garden day and volunteer session. Join us for hands-on community service open to all levels to help care for the garden, along with fun garden activities. We will have a kids' art station and teach visitors how to make their own smudge sticks to take home, using the same plant we will be weeding. This garden is creating space for those who are curious and willing to dig in. Join us! No prior experience necessary - beginners are welcome! GreenThumb will provide supplies and facilitation.This is a great opportunity to support and join a dynamic community space while learning about community gardens. Please wear comfortable, weather-appropriate clothing and bring water and snacks. Note: Some tasks may involve lifting heavy materials and extended periods of bending, squatting, and moving.Register here! Join our second event on August 15th here! Location: 3-22 Beach 84th St, Rockaway Beach, NY 11693For information about group volunteering, please contact Volunteer Program Coordinator, Olivia Navarro at Olivia.Navarro@parks.nyc.govThis event is co-hosted by NYC Parks GreenThumb. We are proud to be the nation’s largest urban community gardening program, sustaining over 550 gardens throughout New York City. With GreenThumb’s support, community gardens are centers of environmental justice, ensuring that our city is fairer, safer, and greener for all. GreenThumb’s events are free and open to the public.

name: Marine Park Forest Restoration
description: Volunteer with the Stewardship team to protect the forested areas in Marine Park! Volunteers will contribute to ongoing restoration work and learn the importance of species diversity to ecosystem health. Come dressed in sturdy boots or shoes, long sleeves, long pants, and clothing that can get dirty. Face masks may be worn at your discretion. Space is limited and registration is required. Volunteers under 18 must be accompanied by a chaperone. This event has been funded in part by NYC Service. To sign up a group of 10 or more volunteers please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov.
location_name: 36th Street and Avenue U (in Marine Park)

name: Trees Count at Jennifer's Playground
description: Volunteer with NYC Parks for Trees Count 2025! At each event you will receive a tote bag with all the materials needed to count trees. Next, we'll provide a 30-minute training session that includes the basics of tree identification and how to measure and assess each tree. Using our Tree Census app, you’ll split up into different zones within the park to start counting trees! After completing your first training event, you are welcome to continue counting trees on your own in smaller parks across the city. For your comfort and safety, please bring water and wear closed-toe shoes to this program. To sign up a group of 10 or more volunteers please reach out to stewardship@parks.nyc.gov. Parking- There are two parking lots near the Visitor Services Booth, P3 and P4, where volunteers can park. For your comfort and safety, please bring water and wear closed-toe shoes to this program.

name: Beginner Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Beginner Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all. Experience a fun and light-hearted, beginner flow that will move you seamlessly from Sun Salutation to a final peak pose. Please bring a mat. The class meets on the lawn north of Sobelsohn Basketball Court.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Outdoor Fitness,Yoga & Pilates Classes

name: 8/1 Forest Restoration at Strack Pond
description: Join NYC Parks and The Forest Park Trust for Forest Restoration this summer to engage in hands-on forest restoration work, including invasive species removal, trail restoration, and possible planting in our park.Registration is required. All tools will be provided, and no prior experience is needed. Just bring your enthusiasm. Please come dressed in sturdy, closed-toe shoes and bring your own water bottle.

name: Summer Sports Experience: Basketball: Skills and Drills
description: Summer Sports Experience is an instructional youth program that teaches sports skills. Our knowledgeable NYC Parks staff train kids in sports such as basketball, ball hockey, kickball, flag football, pickleball, soccer, team handball, track and field, and volleyball. This program covers basic and more advanced techniques. Summer Sports Experience is a drop-in program recommended for children ages 8-14. Participants can stop by for one or more sessions, but kids who stay for the whole program will get the most benefit from it. Occasionally a Summer Sports Experience program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions.Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program. Summer Sports Experience is not a childcare program, and NYC Parks is not responsible for the well-being of attendees. For more information, please visit nyc.gov/parks. Contact accessibility@parks.nyc.gov or (212) 360-1430 for more information regarding accessibility.

name: Shoreline Cleanup
description: Inwood Canoe Club and Friends of Inwood Hill Park are recruiting volunteers to clean up our beloved Hudson River shoreline. We'll be collecting trash to keep our waterfront community clean. Trash bags, work gloves, latex gloves, and grabbers will be provided. Feel free to bring your own work gloves and water bottle.Be sure to complete your 2026 season waiver before you arrive at the boathouse. Inwood Canoe Club en asociación con Friends of Inwood Hill Park están reclutando voluntarios para limpiar nuestra querida orilla del río Hudson. Recogeremos basura para limpiar nuestra comunidad litoral. Proporcionaremos bolsas de basura, guantes de trabajo, junto con algunos guantes de látex y agarradores. Puedes traer tus propios guantes de trabajo y una botella de agua.No olvides completar tu formulario Smart Waiver antes de venir a Inwood Canoe Club.
location_name: Inwood Canoe Club (in Inwood Hill Park)

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. *Participants must bring a lock and lock up all personal belongings.
location_name: Rock Climbing Wall Room (in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center)
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Bodyweight Burn Circuit Training
description: A bodyweight circuit class combining cardio, strength, and core-focused movements for a dynamic full-body workout! This class welcomes all fitness levels.
location_name: Brooklyn Public Library - Brooklyn Heights (in Cadman Plaza Park)

name: LaBlast® Fitness
description: This dance fitness program is based on ballroom dances, including those seen on Dancing with the Stars. This workout in disguise blends dance patterns, fitness, and music to create a low impact workout suitable for all levels.

name: Dance Fitness
description: Join Shape Up NYC for a free dance fitness class every Saturday at West Harlem Piers Park! The instructor for this class will change week to week. This summer the following instructors will be featured: Donnette "Sonnia" McFarlane, Claudia Gomez, Taneka Teel, Beverly Brown, Carolina Morales, Yhajira Muniz, Masayo Kado, Nathalie Roussel, Regina Nicosia, Erica Zhang, and Alejandra Villadiego. This class is appropriate for all fitness levels.
location_name: 125th Street and Marginal Street (in West Harlem Piers)
secondary_categories: Fitness,Shape Up NYC,Outdoor Fitness,Dance Classes,Exercise Classes

name: Basic Canoeing
description: Few experiences compare with being on the open water in New York City. The rhythm of the waves, the sun on your face, and the exhilaration of slicing through the water all add up to an experience you’ll never forget. Our trained Urban Park Rangers will lead you on canoe adventures that range from the gentle waters of protected lakes to the challenging open waters of rivers and bays. All canoe programs are for ages 8 years and older. Meet at Cloverdale Boulevard entrance near 46th Avenue.

name: Salt Marsh Ecology Hike
description: Join the Urban Park Rangers for an interactive walk exploring how the landscape and ecology of Marine Park has been shaped by landfill. Learn about the efforts made to mitigate climate change, conserve natural resources and ecosystem services.
location_name: Salt Marsh Nature Center (in Marine Park)

name: Total Body Reset
description: This fun low-impact full-body workout includes cardio, strength and balance exercises to improve your overall fitness.Class will meet on the Greene Avenue lawn area.

name: Community Paddle at Starlight Park
description: Join the Bronx River Alliance for a free community paddle at Starlight Park! Come see your neighborhood from the river’s perspective!This event is first come, first served with no pre-registration available.“Waves” of paddlers will enjoy the river for about 30-40 minutes, after signing waivers and receiving a brief paddling safety orientation. A total of 4 waves of participants will be able to go on-water every hour of the event (11am, 12pm, 1pm, 2pm), with a maximum of about 15 participants per wave.Staff and volunteers will be on-water to ensure safety, but please keep in mind that this is not a guided tour of the river.Minors must be accompanied by an adult. Waivers for minors will need to be signed by an adult guardian.

name: Phoenix Community Garden Farm Stand
description: Phoenix Community Garden's Farm Stand is open for the season, every Saturday rain or shine until mid-November. Each bag contains local farm fresh fruit, salad greens, the best seasonal vegetables, and aromatics and herbs for flavor. There is sliding scale pricing. Cards, tap to pay and EBT are accepted at the register. Learn more and pre-order on the garden's website here.Pick up at Phoenix Community Garden, 24 Somers Street, between 11:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m.

name: Indigenous Art Workshop with NAIC-NY
description: Join the the North American Indigenous Center of New York and the Bronx River Alliance for an Indigenous Art Workshop at Starlight Park!This family and kid-friendly event will offer participants an opportunity to learn traditional indigenous crafts, and gain exposure to contemporary indigenous cultures.This activity is first come, first served.This project is made possible with funds from the Statewide Community Regrants Program, a regrant program of the New York State Council on the Arts with support of the Office of the Governor and the New York State Legislature and administered by the Bronx Council on the Arts.

name: Incense and Smoke Wand Workshop with Lashette Williams
description: Learn from Lashette how to make loose incense and smoke wands with local plants grown at the Bronx River Foodway. Participants will be introduced to the history and uses of plants and make their own incense blends.Note: This is one of this year’s Community Grant workshops.

name: Ongoing Outdoor Museum Exhibit: Alice's Terracotta Garden
description: Terra cotta sculpture garden featuring antique architectural ornaments from Tottenville's Atlantic Terra Cotta Company (west side lawn of Biddle House).NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States.
location_name: H.H. Biddle House (in Conference House Park)

name: Nature's Workshop: Sandcastles and Beachcombing
description: On National Sandcastle Day, explore how sand and water work together to make strong sandcastles and try fun experiments on the beach. We’ll also go beachcombing along the shoreline to look for shells, stones, and other natural treasures while learning how tides and waves shape what we find. It’s a day of sandy science, shoreline discovery and hands‑on learning.
location_name: Entrance - Cornelia Avenue and Chester Avenue (in Wolfe's Pond Park)

name: Dance in the Park: Merengue
description: Bring your energy and get ready to move at Merengue in the Park! Set against the backdrop of the waterfront and summer breeze, this lively class focuses on rhythm, connection, and fun. Participants will learn easy-to-follow steps and enjoy the fast-paced spirit of Merengue with an enthusiastic instructor leading the way. Perfect for all skill levels, this is a great opportunity to try something new or refine your moves. Come early for our 10 AM yoga class to warm up beforehand!Location: East River Picnic AreaFor inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.
location_name: East River Picnic Area (in Randall's Island Park)

name: Turtle Quest: Explore, Observe, Protect with Lizbeth Miron
description: Join Lizbeth for an exploration of the freshwater turtles that reside in the Bronx River in Shoelace Park, while learning about local ecology and conservation. We will learn to identify native and non-native turtle species and use iNaturalist, a community science app, to document wildlife observations. This workshop also includes a park clean-up to help protect turtle habitats and support the health of the Bronx River ecosystem. The goals of the workshop are to increase awareness of urban wildlife, encourage community involvement in conservation, and empower participants to contribute to environmental research and stewardship in their local parks.

name: Best Special Kids: Horticulture and Art Workshop with Gisselle Ramirez
description: Join Giselle of Best Special Kids for this 1.5-2hr workshop that engages children with disabilities and their families in a sensory-friendly horticulture and art experience. Participants will decorate nursery pots and plant herbs or small vegetables, combining creativity with hands-on learning.The workshop is designed to be inclusive and accessible, with adapted materials, visual supports, and flexible participation. Caregivers are encouraged to participate, strengthening family engagement and reinforcing learning at home.Through this workshop, participants will build fine motor and creative skills, learn basic gardening practices, and develop confidence in a supportive environment. The session also introduces families to ongoing community-based, nature-centered programming.Our goal is to create an inclusive space where children can connect with nature, express themselves, and build meaningful skills while fostering a sense of belonging.

name: Cardio Punch
description: Cardio Punch is a total body, non-contact workout fit for all levels. Punch, hook, and kick to motivating, high-energy music that progress into heart-pumping combinations.
location_name: Boardwalk at Beach 60th Street (in Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk)

name: Play Mobile: Equity Playground
description: The Mobile Recreation Program offers organized activities at events that are free and open to the public. These include community festivals, fairs, free programs, and other related events.The Queens Recreation Play Mobile provides free activities for all children. From May to October, our team of Parks Staff travel throughout the borough providing structured and supervised activities. Our van is stocked with a variety of games and toys that are suitable for children, including board games, chalk, balls, crafts, and more.

name: Bodyweight Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full-body workout. Whether you are a beginner who wants to learn proper form or know your way around the gym and looking for a challenge- this class is for you! Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups and will get your heart pumping.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Summer on the Hudson: Billion Oyster Project Presentation
description: Come see and learn about the Billion Oyster Project on the Baylander’s Jungle Deck. It’s the latest effort to restore our harbor!
location_name: On the Pier (in West Harlem Piers)

name: Chair Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Chair Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all- but designed for those who prefer to be seated. Experience a fun and light-hearted class that provides a seated flow. The goal is to raise your heart rate, challenge all muscle groups and improve balance while remaining seated with minimal impact on joints. Enjoy breath work to ensure you leave class feeling challenged and balanced!
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Dance Fitness
description: Dance Fitness is a creative workout that fuses dance and aerobics. The goal is to improve cardio fitness while having fun and getting a full body workout! This class is taught to music with easy-to-follow steps that will surely get your heart pumping!

name: One Book One Bronx book club
description: Come join the garden group at Bronx River Community Garden for a One Book One Bronx event! This is a weekly community space that sparks powerful conversations and celebrates diverse voices. Saturdays, May 23-August 8, registration required.

name: Summer Sports Experience: Basketball Seasonal Clinics at Faber Recreation Center
description: The Summer Sports Experience programs participants receive age-appropriate instruction through organized clinics, skill stations, drills, conditioning, and live game situations led by NYC Parks Staff.
location_name: Multi-Use Room B (in Faber Pool and Park)

name: Shea Butter Grooves
description: This multi-genre dance class in celebration of joy across the African diaspora. We’ll move through the infectious rhythms of the Caribbean, the pulsing energy of Detroit-style house music, the funk and attitude of hip hop, and the feel-good grooves of reggae and R&B. Come ready to sweat, let loose, and connect with movement that honors the vibrancy and resilience of diaspora culture worldwide. All levels welcome—just bring an open heart and your energy!

name: Yoga
description: This open level class is designed to awaken your mind, body and senses through controlled breathing, simple meditation, and the adoption of specific bodily postures. Please bring a mat and a lock to lock up your bag. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes,Yoga & Pilates Classes

name: Basketball Clinic at Highbridge Recreation Center (Ages: 5 to 7)
description: This Instructional basketball clinic will provide an opportunity for youth, regardless of skill to have a positive basketball experience followed by league play. Participants will have the opportunity to learn and practice the fundamentals of Basketball and advance their basketball skills through a series of drills. This specific clinic for youth ages 5 and 7.

name: Hula Hoop Aerobics
description: his creative and fun workout fuses high-energy dance aerobics with hula hoops! Hoops are available for loaning. Participants must bring waist belts (waist trainers). Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. Center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except; towel, water and phone.
location_name: Multi-Use Room B (in Marcus Garvey Park)
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Dance Classes,Exercise Classes

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. Mats are available for those who need one. Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
location_name: Dance Room (in Constance Baker Motley Recreation Center)
secondary_categories: Shape Up NYC,Recreation Center Programming,Exercise Classes

name: Magnetic Archery and Foam Axe Throwing
description: Our free archery class is a bullseye! Learn the art of archery with free lessons taught by our expert Urban Park Rangers. This program uses safe equipment with magnetic arrows and targets. All equipment will be provided. Foam axe throwing will be available for ages 5 and over.

name: Historic New York: The Little Red Lighthouse
description: The Jeffrey's Hook lighthouse, erected in 1880 and moved to its current site in 1921, has become widely known as the children's literary landmark "The Little Red Lighthouse and the Great Gray Bridge." Rediscover the history of this unique landmark. Open access between 1:00pm and 2:45pm. Participants may need to queue for access due capacity limits inside of the structure. Enter park via overpass closest to Plaza Lafayette. (West 181st Street) and Riverside Drive.
location_name: Little Red Lighthouse (in Fort Washington Park)

name: Park Open Studio
description: Led by a local teaching artist, this drop-in class invites participants to explore creativity through hands-on artmaking under the shade of Scrabble Plaza’s trees. Past mediums have included plein-air drawing, printmaking, cyanotypes, and more. All materials are provided by the Washington Square Park Conservancy.
location_name: Northwest Corner Seating Area (in Washington Square Park)

name: All That Skitters!
description: How does the Fiddler Crab survive in the salt marsh tides? What other crabs live in the marsh? What happens in the winter? Where do Horseshoe Crabs fit in? Explore the complexities and threats to the fragile salt marsh with Mike Feller, retired Chief Naturalist of the NYC Parks Department. Sponsored by the Salt Marsh Alliance in partnership with the Marine Park Alliance
location_name: Salt Marsh Nature Center (in Marine Park)

name: Basketball Clinic at Highbridge Recreation Center (Ages: 8 to 12)
description: This Instructional basketball clinic will provide an opportunity for youth, regardless of skill to have a positive basketball experience followed by league play. Participants will have the opportunity to learn and practice the fundamentals of Basketball and advance their basketball skills through a series of drills. This specific clinic for youth ages 8 and 12.

name: Public Gallery Tour
description: Solo and group exhibitions at Glyndor Gallery explore the dynamic relationships between nature, culture, and site. When exhibitions are on view in the spring, summer, and fall, Gallery Greeters lead public exhibition tours on Thursdays and Saturdays at 2PM. Visitors can expect an in-depth look at artworks on view in Glyndor Gallery and in the Sunroom Project Space, showcasing the work of both emerging and established artists in the unique context of a public garden. Each tour lasts approximately 45 minutes. Drop-in event; no registration required. Today’s tour features current exhibitions. Questions? Please email us at information@wavehill.org or call 718.549.3200 x251. Glyndor Gallery is wheelchair-accessible. There is an accessible, ground-level entrance at the front of the building with an elevator that provides access to the gallery level. The Sunroom Project Space can be accessed with an ADA-compliant ramp. The restroom on the gallery level is all-gender and ADA-compliant.

name: Garden Glow-Up: Plant-Based Beauty Workshop
description: Discover how plants can nourish us inside and out! This hands-on workshop explores simple, plant-based beauty practices using natural ingredients inspired by the garden. Participants will learn how herbs, flowers, and everyday plants can support self-care, wellness, and creativity. All attendees must fill out the Saturdays on the Farm registration and complete the farm waiver: https://www.harlemgrown.org/saturdays-on-the-farm

name: Summer on the Hudson: Story Hour!
description: Weekly story time for kids featuring rotating books and guest readers. Events take place on Saturdays in May, June, August and September. No events May 16 or September 12.

name: Seining
description: Our experienced Urban Park Rangers teach the ecology of our waterways on every seining program. Get ready to get your hands wet; seining programs use nets to catch fish. All equipment is provided; this program is recommended for ages 8 years and older. Participation is first-come, first-served.
location_name: Entrance - East 88th Street and Seaview Avenue (in Canarsie Park)

name: Cocktails on the Farm
description: Enjoy a relaxed evening at Randall’s Island Park with Cocktails on our Urban Farm!Sip on farm-fresh cocktails (21+) or complimentary mocktails, while enjoying plant-based bites, crafts, and activities. Plus, bring your clothing and textiles that need repair for a hands-on mending workshop with Repair Cafe El Barrio!Afterwards, join us for Movie Night! Bring a blanket and settle in under the stars for an outdoor movie night in the Park. Enjoy sweeping views of the Hell Gate Bridge and the city skyline while watching Zootopia 2, the perfect pick for a fun summer evening!Location: Urban FarmFor inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.

name: Summer on the Hudson: Silent Disco
description: Grab headphones and party on the Pier at this Quiet Clubbing event featuring three live DJs playing different genres. Arrive early! Line opens at 5:30pm. Headphone supplies are limited and there may be a wait. Rain date September 19.

name: It's My Park at Leif Ericson Park
description: This It's My Park season, volunteer with Parent-Child Relationship Association to beautify Leif Ericson Park. We look forward to cleaning up the park with you!

name: Hudson Classical Theater Company presents: The Dancing Men
description: The Hudson Classical Theater Company Presents The Dancing Men: A Sherlock Holmes Mystery written by Susane Lee, adapted from the short story by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, and directed by Susane Lee and Nicholas Martin-Smith.Hilton Cubitt seeks the help of Sherlock Holmes when he comes across a series of mysterious stick figure drawings resembling “dancing men” about his garden. These drawings are linked to his wife, Elsie Patrick, an American woman who harbors a secret past. Holmes needs to decipher these "dancing men" before tragedy strikes. The Dancing Men runs July 23rd - August 16th. All shows are Thursdays to Sundays at 6:30pm. No reservations--arrive by 6:15pm for a good seat. Cushions provided. Hudson Classical Theater Company Shows are “Pay What You Can.” Audience donations help pay for artists stipends and production costs. Directions to The Soldiers’ & Sailors’ Monument:Subways: Take the 1 to West 86 or West 96, or take the 2 to West 96. Walk west towards Riverside Park. You can enter the park at West 89th street or take the entrance at West 91st Street. You will see the Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Monument. Take the path to the right of the monument (not the one closest to the river). Walk around the path and you will be BEHIND the monument where audiences will be seated.
location_name: Soldiers' and Sailors' Monument (in Riverside Park)

name: Cinema Under the Stars: The Price of the Ticket (1989) by Karen Thorsen
description: AFF’s 2026 Summer Series presents The Price of the Ticket with an introduction by the Director Karen Thorsen. The film is preceded by James Baldwin: From Another Place. Live jazz + film under the stars! Join us for a special evening of film, music, and community in St. Nicholas Park as part of AFF’s 2026 Summer Series. Festivities begin at 6:30 PM with live jazz by David Lee Jones & The Harlem Groove Band. At 8:15 PM, we’ll screen James Baldwin: From Another Place then The Price of the Ticket, a revelatory documentary showcasing archival footage and associates of James Baldwin. This emotional film expounds on Baldwin’s legacy with appearances from Maya Angelou, Amiri Baraka, William Stryton, and others. Bring your blankets, lawn chairs, and loved ones for a night under the stars. The program is co-presented by Maysles Cinema, Harlem One Stop, and NYC Parks.Free and open to all. In case of rain, the screening will take place at Maysles Cinema.
location_name: James Baldwin Lawn (in St. Nicholas Park),135th Street and St. Nicholas Avenue (in St. Nicholas Park)

name: RTA Summer Sunset Concert Series featuring The Brooklyners
description: Waterfront sunset concerts sponsored by the Riverside Tennis Association. No performance on July 4th. June 20 - Ron McClure & Friends June 27 - Morrisania Band Project July 11 - The Afro-LatineersJuly 18 - The Slim Kings July 25 - Richard Baratta August 1 - The BrooklynersAugust 8 - DurieuxThe Brooklyners is a NYC reggae band that blends Jamaican standards, French classics, and original conscious tunes into a unique tropical cocktail. The band got its name from its members’ diversity and multicultural backgrounds, as well as from its home base of Brooklyn, NYC. Formed 10 years ago, the group has since played hundreds of shows in NYC and traveled up and down the East Coast.The Brooklyners features David Langlois on vocals, percussion, and rhythm guitar; Ras Abda G on vocals and bass; Kikkah Jackson on vocals and drums; Andy Bassford on guitar; and Bryan Benhingove on keyboards, saxophone, and melodica

name: Wheel Fun Rentals Celebrates America's 250th with Patriotic LED Bike Ride
description: Wheel Fun Rentals at Boathouse Row is lighting up summer nights in patriotic style as it celebrates America's 250th anniversary with special Red, White & Blue LED Illuminated Surrey Night Rides. Patriotic Surrey Nights Rides are available now and will run through Labor Day Weekend at their Bensonhurst Park and FDR Boardwalk at Sand Lane bike rental locations.For the summer-long celebration, Wheel Fun Rentals has transformed its popular LED-illuminated specialty bikes into dazzling patriotic attractions featuring custom red, white, and blue lighting. Families, couples, and friends can cruise along two of New York City's scenic waterfronts while celebrating America's historic 250th birthday.On July 4th, guests can make the holiday even more memorable by riding to waterfront viewing areas for spectacular fireworks displays. Launching from Bensonhurst Park in Brooklyn, riders can enjoy the festive atmosphere along the waterfront bike path with views across New York Harbor. On Staten Island, guests can pedal along the FDR Boardwalk, where the waterfront provides a scenic setting to take in the evening's Independence Day celebrations and fireworks over the harbor.Night Rides are first-come, first-served and available Friday – Sundays and holidays beginning at sunset until 10pm, last 1-hour rental goes out at 9pm. Pricing is $29 for a Single Surrey (seats up to 3 adults and 2 small children), and $39 for a Double Surrey (seats up to 6 adults and 2 small children).For additional location information, current hours of operation, and to learn more about Wheel Fun Rentals available recreational rental options, visit wheelfunrentals.com/NYC.
location_name: Meadow Lake North (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park),Sand Lane and Father Capadanno Boulevard (in Franklin D. Roosevelt Boardwalk and Beach),Marine Park,Bensonhurst Park,David Dinkins Circle (in Flushing Meadows Corona Park)
address: Flushing Meadows Corona Park, Franklin D. Roosevelt Boardwalk and Beach, Marine Park, Bensonhurst Park
